results.title: Apple sues OpenAI as its own suppliers Foxconn and Luxshare line up behind a rival device
results.description: Apple's decision to sue OpenAI for trade-secret theft does more than escalate a soured partnership between two of technology's most influential companies. It injects legal uncertainty into OpenAI's push to build its own consumer hardware — an effort a growing roster of Asian suppliers has already begun to serve, and it lands just as the AI developer approaches a closely watched initial public offering.

results.title: Medicare’s new approach to halting fraud is paying off
results.description: Last year, we wrote in the Los Angeles Times that the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services was done chasing criminals after we’d already handed them money. We said we were building something different — a prevention-first operation that would detect and stop fraud before the check cleared, not attempt to claw back lost funds years later. Skeptics had reason to doubt. Government agencies announce transformations all the time. The results usually don’t follow. So here are the numbers: In fiscal year 2025, Medicare savings from the prevention of fraud, waste and abuse hit $42 billion — an almost 60%...

results.title: Breaking Down the Earnings Catalyst and What It Really Signals for Markets
results.description: Every seasoned investor has experienced it — the moment a single earnings report transforms a company’s stock trajectory overnight. That phenomenon has a name: the earnings catalyst. It is one of the most powerful forces in financial markets, capable of rewarding patient shareholders handsomely or punishing the unprepared with brutal speed. Understanding what drives these... The post Breaking Down the Earnings Catalyst and What It Really Signals for Markets appeared first on Equity Insider .

results.title: 2007 Bentley Continental Flying Spur
results.description: This 2007 Bentley Continental Flying Spur was specified in Chestnut Marrone Metallic over a light brown and dark brown leather-trimmed interior, and it has heated and ventilated massaging front seats, heated and ventilated rear seats, a rear-seat entertainment system, rear picnic tables, and four-zone automatic climate control. Power comes from a twin-turbocharged 6.0-liter W12 that drives all four wheels through a six-speed automatic transaxle. Additional appointments include a Torsen center differential, a glass sunroof, bi-xenon headlights, soft-close doors, parking sensors, chrome-finished 19′′ five-spoke alloy wheels, and air suspension. The car was sold on BaT in April 2026 and subsequently was reacquired by the selling dealer. This Flying Spur has 58k miles and is offered in Florida with a clean Carfax report and a clean Minnesota title.

results.title: Ivan Herrera earns first All-Star selection after Shohei Ohtani withdrawal
results.description: The St. Louis Cardinals are sending another player to the 2026 MLB All-Star Game. After Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani withdrew from the Midsummer Classic because of knee irritation, Cardinals designated hitter and catcher Ivan Herrera was selected as his replacement, giving the 26-year-old the first All-Star honor of his MLB career. It's a well-deserved recognition for one of the National League's most productive offensive catchers, and it gives St. Louis another representative during what has been an encouraging first half of the season.
